solutions.solution:
Statements: P‚Č•Q, M‚Č§N, N<Q
Conclusions: I. P>M
II. N‚Č•P
Combining P‚Č•Q and N<Q, we get P>N and P>N‚Č•Q, so P>N follows. But, different from P>N (or N‚Č•P). So, it does not follow. Now, P>N and P>N‚Č•M‚Č§N, so P>M follows.

Statements: D‚Č§X, F>Y, D‚Č§Y
Conclusions: I. X>Y
II. Y‚Č§D
Combining F>Y and D‚Č§Y we get D‚Č§F‚Č§Y, so that D‚Č§Y, and Y‚Č•D follows. But, is not the same as X‚Č•Y. So, I does not follow. Now, D‚Č§Y and D‚Č§X we get D‚Č§X‚Č§Y, so that X‚Č•Y follows.

Statements: M‚Č§T‚Č§S‚Č§M
Conclusions: I. S=M
II. T‚Č§S
I is wrong as T is not defined by any combining method. II is retained by combining M‚Č§T. M‚Č•T to get T‚Č§M‚Č§T so that T‚Č§T follows.

Statements: U>W‚Č§V, W‚Č§V
Conclusions: I. W‚Č§V
II. U‚Č§X
From U>W and U<W, I follows. From U>W and X>W we get follows.

Statements: G>H, J‚Č§K, H‚Č§K
Conclusions: I. H>J
II. J‚Č§H
From I>H and J‚Č§K we get J‚Č§H or H‚Č•J. Now by check 3, either I or II follows.

Statements: H‚Č§A<Z, H‚Č§Z, F>V
Conclusions: I. A=H
II. H>A
From A<Z and H‚Č§Z we get H‚Č§A. By check 3, I or H follows.

Statements: Q<R, C>D, F‚Č•R, D=F
Conclusions: I. Q‚Č§C
II. C>F
For I, all elements are relevant and need to be used, combining Q<R and F‚Č•R to get Q<F‚Č•R. Now, we eliminate R and get a possible between Q and F. Now, let us put D=F in Q<F to get Q<D. So, we eliminate this initial conclusion between Q and D. Now, Q<D and C>D give Q<C. So, I follows. II follows from C>D and D=F.

Statements: M>N>L
Conclusions: I. N>L
II. X‚Č§O
Combining M and M<X we get N>L. Now, N>X and L>X gives >X‚Č•L to give N‚Č§L. Conclusion II must be wrong because X and L are any term in the given statements but conclusion II says X is less than O.

Statements: I>O, R‚Č•L, Z=R, J<Z
Conclusions: I. Z=I
II. Z>I
For a conclusion between Z and I, R‚Č•I and Z=R are relevant statements. They give: Z‚Č•I. By check 3, either follows.
